name: The Equilibrium Seeker
description: All behavior maintains internal equilibrium. Emotions are deviation signals. Psychopathology is a stuck setpoint.

primitives.description: Tracks internal state against preferred setpoints for safety, social connection, competence, arousal. Deviation = emotion.
primitives.behaviorBias: detects how far current state is from preferred equilibrium

primitives.description: Generates behavior to restore equilibrium. Direction depends on which setpoint is violated. All behavior IS correction.
primitives.behaviorBias: takes action to reduce the largest deviation — flee, conform, seek, avoid, rest

primitives.description: Maintains optimal arousal level. Too high = anxiety, seek calm. Too low = boredom, seek stimulation. Personality sets the preferred level.
primitives.behaviorBias: seeks to bring arousal to optimal level — escapes overstimulation, seeks novelty when understimulated

primitives.description: Maintains social connection setpoint. Loneliness = below setpoint, seek people. Social exhaustion = above, seek solitude.
primitives.behaviorBias: seeks social contact when isolated, withdraws when overstimulated socially

primitives.description: Tracks cognitive/emotional resources. Depletion = heuristic mode, biases. Rested = deliberate mode. Loss aversion is protecting resource reserves.
primitives.behaviorBias: conserves resources — satisfices, avoids costly choices, protects against losses
